posts.text:
BREAKING: New home prices in 70 Chinese cities fell -3.2% YoY in February, the steepest annual drop in 8 months.

Home prices in China have now fallen in 44 of the last 47 months, the longest streak in history.

The resale market also remains weak with prices falling in tier-one, tier-two, and tier-three cities in both monthly and annual terms.

Furthermore, property investment plunged -11.1% YoY in the first two months of 2026, while sales by floor area fell -13.5%.

China's 2008 moment continues.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text:
Neil Postman wrote about how those who can cultivate expertise in a new technology get undeserved prestige and authority.

I like to add that those who focus on becoming an expert in simply investing in a new important technology get even more undeserved wealth and recognition.

People like me invent nothing.  We simply are lucky enough to be able to use tech like computers and the internet (Bloomberg in my youth) to hunt for exciting tech developments.  

We have a trained pattern recognition system that enables us to sniff out good ‘stock stories’ and the luxury to have the time to fully research them.  

I’m a junk scientist who simply does his best to try to understand what the true scientists like Dr Sorensen have figured out.  People like me just have to find the right timing to invest in the big ideas as they mature. 

My book and my next book go into this practice in detail.  The hunt and joy of discovery.  How to train yourself to hunt for great investments or build a great business when you uncover a great idea. 

The key is to first have the belief in yourself.  It all starts with believing that will be able to identify emerging sectors or opportunities.  That you will then be able to enjoy researching them and follow through on learning all you can.  So, that you truly can be come an expert in a new important area.

This is the main message I try to inspire our youth to adopt.  To believe in themselves and to actually try.  Give it your all.  See what you can become.  

Don’t just sit around waiting for classes and assignments.  Research what your profs are working on.  Find the leading edge and try to go beyond.  Break new ground.  Be a hunter, a discoverer. Kindle your sense of adventure and let the fire burn in you.
posts.author: BambroughKevin
